Reviews ArticlesCarveyance

New 2025 Plug-In Hybrid SUVs to meet your every demand.

Dec 12

This is more informational rather than a ranking as each SUV fits a certain lifestyle, budget, and so what is right for you may not be right for another person who lives elsewhere, this is to show you the options that are out there and how they might appeal to you. All reviews and data were done by professionals.

Switching to a new type of vehicle can feel like a significant leap for many consumers, especially when it involves rethinking how they power or fuel their vehicles. Fortunately, there are transitional options between traditional internal combustion engine run vehicles (ICE) and fully electric vehicles (EVs), such as hybrids and plug-in hybrids.

A plug-in hybrid strikes a balance between gas-powered and electric vehicles, featuring a hybrid powertrain with a slightly larger battery than standard hybrids. This allows for limited driving on electric power alone. By plugging it in, you can enjoy an electric-only range for shorter trips, while the gasoline engine extends the total range when needed. This flexibility makes plug-in hybrids particularly well-suited for sport utility vehicles, offering both efficiency and versatility.

A plug-In hybrid is often seen as the best of both worlds. It combines the convenience of a traditional hybrid with power and range of a gas-powered engine relieving any range anxiety one might have. Much like a fully electric vehicle, you have the ability of plugging it in to charge or you can have the gas-powered engine charge it just by driving it. For everyday errands or commutes, you can rely on the electric power alone. For longer trips or when maximum power and range are required, the gas engine seamlessly takes over, working in tandem with the battery. This makes plug-in hybrids a practical and adaptable choice for those not ready to make the full jump to electric yet.

Blend of Performance, Luxury, and Practicality: Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV

We start off with the very underrated but accomplished Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV. Mitsubishi and especially the Outlander are not new to the Plug-In hybrid world. Among 22 vehicles, ranging from the Range Rover Cullinan to the Subaru Crosstrek, the 2023 NWAPA Mudfest crowned the Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV as its overall winner. It earned the prestigious titles of Northwest Outdoor Activity Vehicle of the Year and Best Three-Row Family SUV of the Year.

The Outlander PHEV was celebrated for its impressive handling, refined interior, and ability to tackle uneven terrain with ease. As a family-focused compact SUV with a standard third row, it positions itself as a serious competitor to the Toyota RAV4 Prime. Offering 38 miles of electric-only range (compared to the RAV4 Prime’s 42) and a more upscale interior, the Outlander PHEV strikes a balance between style and substance. Its total range extends to around 420 miles on a full tank and the battery can be charged to 80% in just 25 minutes with a DC fast charger, making it a practical and stylish option for adventurous families.

With 248 horsepower and 332 lb-ft of torque, the PHEV is significantly more powerful than its gas-powered counterpart, which delivers 181 horsepower and torque. The electric motors are the stars of the show, handling most of the driving, while the gas engine supplements by powering the front wheels when needed. At low speeds, the Outlander PHEV operates much like an electric vehicle, offering a nearly one-pedal regenerative braking mode that slows the vehicle down to about 5 mph.

Mitsubishi's Super-All Wheel Control (S-AWC) all-wheel-drive system comes standard, along with seven drive modes, four of which cater specifically to terrain challenges: Tarmac, Gravel, Deep Snow, and Mud. These capabilities, combined with seamless transitions between gas and electric power, make the Outlander PHEV adept at conquering slippery surfaces.

Excelling both on the pavement and in off-road conditions, the Mitsubishi Outlander PHEV showcases an exceptional blend of performance, luxury, and practicality. New for 2025, the model now includes heated front seats as standard equipment across all trims. The mid-tier SEL trim sees notable upgrades as well, adding a standard heated steering wheel and a panoramic moonroof, enhancing both comfort and luxury for drivers and passengers alike. It’s a standout in the plug-in hybrid SUV category and a must-consider for anyone seeking versatility and capability in one package.

Starting at $41,940

⚡

2025 Mercedes-Benz GLE Plug-In Hybrid: Sophistication Meets Sustainability

The 2025 Mercedes-Benz GLE Plug-In Hybrid continues to elevate the balance between luxury, technology, and eco-conscious performance. Positioned as a standout in the premium SUV segment, the GLE Plug-In Hybrid offers remarkable efficiency without compromising on the signature comfort and refinement that Mercedes-Benz is known for.

The 2025 GLE Plug-In Hybrid is equipped with a robust 2.0-liter turbocharged four-cylinder engine paired with an electric motor. Together, they produce a combined output of 381 horsepower and 479 lb-ft of torque, delivering an exhilarating driving experience. The advanced hybrid powertrain is mated to a smooth 9-speed automatic transmission and the brand's 4MATIC all-wheel-drive system, ensuring stability and agility in a variety of driving conditions. What sets the 2025 GLE apart is its impressive electric-only range, which has been extended to 48 miles thanks to a larger 31.2 kWh battery pack. For urban commutes or short trips, the GLE can function as a fully electric vehicle, while the gas engine ensures long-distance capability with a total range exceeding 500 miles. Its on-board 11 kW charger enables faster charging, and DC fast-charging capability (up to 60 kW) allows the battery to recharge to 80% in just 20 minutes.

Step inside the GLE Plug-In Hybrid, and you'll find a meticulously crafted cabin filled with premium materials and advanced technology. The MBUX infotainment system takes center stage, with a 12.3-inch touchscreen and an equally large digital instrument cluster. The system includes voice control, navigation with augmented reality, wireless Apple CarPlay, Android Auto, and seamless integration with the Mercedes me app.

For 2025, the GLE now features heated front seats as standard, with the mid-level trims adding a heated steering wheel and a panoramic moonroof. Optional packages include luxury upgrades like ventilated seats, massaging functions, and the ENERGIZING Comfort system, which combines lighting, music, and seat functions to create a personalized wellness experience.

Starts at an estimated $73,000

⚡

2025 Jeep Grand Cherokee 4xe: Where Luxury and Off-Road prowess meet Efficiency

The Jeep Grand Cherokee has long been around since 1993 and a benchmark in the midsize SUV segment. Now with the advent of the plug-in hybrid 4xe model, Jeep has taken a giant leap into the electrified future. The 2025 Jeep Grand Cherokee 4xe represents the perfect blend of off-road capability, luxury, and eco-friendly innovation, solidifying its place as one of the most versatile SUVs in its class.

The Grand Cherokee 4xe continues to utilize Jeep’s robust plug-in hybrid powertrain from last year with a 2.0-liter turbocharged inline-four cylinder engine, paired with two electric motors and 17.3-kWh battery pack, when combined together it produces 375 horsepower and 470 lb-ft of torque which adds up to an impressive 6,000lbs of towing. That is significantly more power and torque from previous Jeep models sporting a V8. Ensuring smooth shifts is an 8-speed automatic transmission whether you are on cruising down the freeway or tackling the open trail, the power is never an issue. In addition you have Jeep’s legendary Quadra-Drive II four-wheel-drive system standard, adjustable ride height, and the five drive modes; Rock, Sand/Mud, Snow, Auto, and Electric modes all to adapt to the terrain ahead providing unparalleled traction and stability in any condition.

The 4xe’s hybrid system enables impressive versatility. It can drive on pure electric power for shorter commutes up to 26 miles or switch to hybrid mode for long-distance efficiency. Without electric power, the Grand Cherokee 4xe achieves a respectable combined 23 mpg. When utilizing its plug-in hybrid system, it delivers an impressive EPA-estimated 56 MPGe. Thanks to its quick charging capability—reaching full charge in approximately 2.5 hours with a Level 2 charger with a combined range of nearly 450 miles, effectively eliminating any concerns about range anxiety.

For 2025, Jeep has made several updates to refine the Grand Cherokee 4xe’s already impressive formula. Jeep has introduced a more intuitive infotainment system, featuring a larger 12.3-inch touchscreen, wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto, and enhanced over-the-air updates. The 2025 model also brings new color schemes and sustainable materials for its interior, including optional vegan leather trims to align with its eco-friendly ethos. For those of you more adventurous and want to get your Jeep badges the rugged Trailhawk trim now features a revamped Selec-Terrain system, giving drivers even more control in challenging off-road conditions. Lastly Jeep has made Adaptive cruise control, lane-keeping assist, blind-spot monitoring, and automatic emergency braking standard. A 360 degree camera and semi-autonomous driving system is included, making highway travel smoother and safer.

The 2025 Jeep Grand Cherokee 4xe is an outstanding choice for buyers seeking a plug-in hybrid SUV that doesn’t compromise on performance, luxury, or off-road prowess. With its improved electric range, cutting-edge technology, and iconic Jeep capability, the Grand Cherokee 4xe strikes the perfect balance between adventure and sustainability with its 70.8 of cargo space. Whether you’re commuting in the city, embarking on a weekend camping trip, or simply navigating snowy roads, the Grand Cherokee 4xe is ready for the challenge.

Starts at approximately $61,000.

⚡

2025 Lexus TX 550 Plug-In Hybrid: The Nexus of the Plug-In Hybrids, Boring, Pricey, but Perfect

The Lexus TX Plug-In Hybrid showcases an impressive blend of performance, practicality, and luxury, making it a standout choice in the plug-in hybrid SUV segment, it marks a significant step forward for Lexus in the realm of luxury three-row SUVs with electrified performance. Designed to blend premium comfort, cutting-edge technology, and eco-conscious engineering, the TX PHEV competes in the increasingly crowded plug-in hybrid SUV segment. Despite its size, the TX is surprisingly nimble, offering stable cornering and a mostly serene cabin environment.

Under the hood, the TX Plug-In Hybrid houses a robust 3.5-liter V6 engine paired with two electric motors, delivering a combined 404 horsepower. This powerful setup propels the SUV from 0 to 60 mph in a brisk 5.9 seconds, offering a dynamic driving experience that rivals some sportier SUVs. The ride strikes a fine balance—firm yet comfortable—although the steering lacks the feedback driving enthusiasts might prefer. The standard all-wheel drive system, coupled with a continuously variable automatic transmission (CVT), ensures smooth transitions between gas and electric power while maintaining traction on varying road surfaces. When properly equipped, the TX PHEV can tow up to 5,000 pounds, enhancing its versatility for families with active lifestyles.

Fuel efficiency is a major highlight of the TX Plug-In Hybrid. In gas/hybrid mode, it achieves an astonishing EPA-estimated 29 mpg in the city and 28 mpg on the highway. In electric/hybrid mode, it boasts an impressive 76 MPGe combined, demonstrating its eco-conscious engineering. Even the all-electric range of 33 miles is impressive considering the size and weight of the TX and is ideal for short commutes and errands. Charging is also convenient, with a full charge taking approximately 3 hours using a Level 2 charger or a 240-volt outlet, ensuring minimal downtime between drives.

The TX retains the bold, angular styling that Lexus is known for, with an emphasis on aerodynamics and a commanding road presence. Inside, it delivers the luxury Lexus is known for, with an emphasis on comfort and functionality while featuring a spacious cabin designed to accommodate up to seven passengers comfortably. Premium materials such as semi-aniline leather, open-pore wood trim, and brushed aluminum accents create a luxurious environment. While it may not be the most opulent SUV in its class, the cabin is meticulously crafted and filled with thoughtful touches. Technology takes center stage with a massive touchscreen infotainment system, wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto, and a digital gauge cluster. The three-row configuration accommodates up to six passengers, with a notably spacious and adult using third row—a rarity among midsize luxury SUVs. The first and second rows provide exceptional support and cushioning, ensuring long-distance comfort.

Speaking about space the TX excels in practicality, offering generous cargo space that rivals many competitors. With all three rows in use, there’s 20.2 cubic feet of storage behind the third row plenty for groceries flea market finds. Folding the third row expands the space to 57.4 cubic feet, while lowering both the second and third rows unlocks a cavernous 97 cubic feet, enough to fit a traveling band's equipment. A standard hands-free power liftgate adds convenience, making loading and unloading effortless.

The 2025 Lexus TX Plug-In Hybrid is a versatile and well-rounded SUV that combines luxury, practicality, and efficiency into one compelling package. Whether you’re looking for strong performance, ample cargo capacity, or three rows of family-friendly seating, the TX PHEV delivers in nearly every category. It strikes an ideal balance for advanced hybrid technology, and thoughtful design, and that signature Lexus craftsmanship we all know and love.

Starts at approximately $78,000.

⚡

2025 Ford Escape Plug-In Hybrid: Efficient, Versatile, and Modern

The 2025 Ford Escape Plug-In Hybrid is a versatile and eco-friendly compact SUV that caters to drivers seeking a balance of efficiency, practicality, and modern features. With a refreshed design and advanced hybrid technology, the Escape PHEV continues to be a strong contender in the growing plug-in hybrid market.

Under the hood, the 2025 Ford Escape Plug-In Hybrid is powered by a 2.5-liter Atkinson-cycle four-cylinder engine paired with an electric motor, delivering a combined 210 horsepower. This powertrain provides smooth quick acceleration from a stop but the power gradually diminishes as the SUV reaches higher speeds, ample performance for daily driving, but not much else. Some find the handling bit cumbersome around the corners with steering that is too light and lacks the necessary feedback from the road that one would expect from a vehicle of this size and its predecessors. Its ride is firm due to the well-tuned suspension, though some may find it a bit too stiff for their liking. Oddly enough front-wheel-drive is the only configuration, most likely to keep the weight down and the efficiency up by not offering all-wheel-drive. Speaking of which the fuel efficiency is a standout feature. The Escape PHEV achieves an estimated 40 mpg in hybrid mode. When using electric power alone, it offers an EPA-estimated all-electric range of 37 miles, making it an excellent option for short commutes or running errands without using gas. A full charge can be completed in about 3.5 hours using a Level 2 charger.

The 2025 model brings subtle updates to the Escape's exterior, giving it a sleek and more modern appearance. A refreshed front grille, updated LED lighting, and aerodynamic styling enhance its visual appeal while improving efficiency. The Escape PHEV retains its compact dimensions, making it easy to maneuver in urban settings, parking in tight spaces, and maximizing spatial awareness at every level.

Inside, the Escape PHEV’s cabin quality may lag behind some competitors in its price range, but it compensates with a variety of standard features and a practical design. The interior offers a comfortable and functional layout, accommodating up to five passengers with ease. Adjustable rear seats enhance versatility, providing extra legroom or expanded storage space as needed, making it a well-rounded choice for everyday use. The Escape 34.4 cubic feet of cargo space behind the rear seats, expanding to 60.8 cubic feet with the seats folded flat. This versatile cargo area makes it a practical choice for families or anyone needing extra storage for gear or luggage.

Where the cabin may have suffered the technology has not with Ford's latest SYNC 4 infotainment system, featuring a large touchscreen display with wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto compatibility. Other standard features include a digital instrument cluster, dual-zone climate control, and a suite of driver-assistance technologies under the Ford Co-Pilot360 banner. Optional upgrades, such as a premium sound system and a panoramic sunroof, further enhance the driving experience. Along with technology the Escape prioritizes safety, equipping it with a robust set of standard driver-assistance features. These include adaptive cruise control, lane-keeping assist, blind-spot monitoring, and automatic emergency braking. An available 360-degree camera system adds an extra layer of confidence when navigating tight spaces or parking.

The 2025 Ford Escape Plug-In Hybrid is a well-rounded compact SUV that combines impressive efficiency, practical utility, and modern technology. With its competitive pricing and excellent all-electric range, it’s an appealing choice for environmentally conscious drivers who want to have an entry point into the plug-in hybrid market and a capable and comfortable vehicle for daily driving and beyond. Whether you are tackling city streets or embarking on longer journeys, the Escape PHEV delivers a compelling mix of performance and practicality.

Pricing starts at around $39,500

⚡

2025 Mazda CX-70 PHEV: A Sophisticated Blend of Performance, Refinement, Efficiency, and Fun

Some may argue that the 2025 Mazda CX-70 Plug-In Hybrid (PHEV) is just the CX-90 PHEV without the third row seating option and they would be right, bringing Mazda’s signature driving dynamics, upscale design, and eco-conscious engineering in a versatile midsize SUV. Positioned between the CX-50 and the CX-90, the CX-70 PHEV aims to deliver a premium experience without compromising efficiency or practicality.

At the heart of the CX-70 PHEV is a 2.5-liter four-cylinder engine paired with an electric motor and a robust battery pack. Together, they produce an impressive 323 horsepower and 369 lb-ft of torque, ensuring quick acceleration and confident performance. The powertrain is mated to an eight-speed automatic transmission, a departure from the more common CVTs you will find in many plug-in hybrids, ensuring a more engaging driving experience. While the transmission’s performance might fall short of Mazda’s usual precision, the powertrain overall remains a strong performer for the class. Standard all-wheel drive enhances stability and control, making the CX-70 PHEV well-suited for various road conditions, including mild off-roading. If the engine is the heart the soul is the finely tuned suspension which gracefully balances comfort with agility, delivering the responsive handling that Mazda vehicles are known for. True to Mazda’s ethos, the CX-70 PHEV offers a driving experience that prioritizes engagement what Mazda enthusiasts have come to expect, even with its hefty 5,000-pound curb weight. Its precise, well-weighted steering and sport-tuned suspension provide confidence during sharp maneuvers, always feeling stable and composed even during spirited driving, making it one of the more engaging plug-in hybrids to drive. Whether cruising on highways or navigating winding roads, the CX-70 PHEV delivers a dynamic and enjoyable ride.

As far as efficiency goes the EPA has yet to release the official fuel economy estimates for the CX-70 PHEV. However, based on its similarities to its brother the CX-90 PHEV—which achieves 24 mpg in the city and 27 mpg on the highway—it's reasonable to anticipate comparable figures. This efficient performance would position the CX-70 PHEV as a recommended choice for those seeking a balance of power and fuel economy in a midsize SUV. Keeping track with performance, the 17.8-kWh battery pack equipped on the CX-70 PHEV is capable of recharging from 20% to 80% in approximately 90 minutes using a 240-volt connection. On a full charge 26 miles of all-electric driving range is available, providing an eco-friendly option for shorter trips and daily commutes.

Mazda has always had an eye for fluid design emphasizing more than just a bland shape like most modern SUVs on the market. Its design tells a story of passion, refinement, luxury, and desire and Mazda took that same direction with the interior for the CX-70 PHEV. True to Mazda’s reputation, the cabin features upscale materials and a minimalist design approach, creating an inviting atmosphere. Soft-touch surfaces, optional leather upholstery, and tasteful accents contribute to the upscale vibe, while excellent build quality ensures durability. It comfortably accommodates up to five passengers, with supportive seats designed for long drives. The front seats are both spacious and ergonomically crafted, providing ample adjustments for the ideal driving position. Rear-seat passengers also benefit from generous legroom and headroom, making the CX-70 suitable for families and fun road trips. Split-folding rear seats add flexibility for cargo storage. Available features like heated and ventilated front seats, a panoramic sunroof, and premium leather upholstery elevate the comfort level. In the center of all of this is Mazda’s intuitive infotainment system, a large 12.3-inch display, controlled via a rotary dial vs touchscreen. Standard features include wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto, a 12-speaker Bose audio system, and a suite of connected services. The driver-centric layout ensures that all controls are within easy reach, enhancing the overall user experience.

Continuing with safety and technology Mazda has equipped the CX-70 PHEV with an extensive suite of advanced safety and driver-assistance features. Standard on every model is Mazda’s i-Activsense system, which includes adaptive cruise control, lane-keeping assist, blind-spot monitoring, and automatic emergency braking. Optional features, such as a 360-degree camera and traffic jam assist, provide added confidence on the road.

As you can imagine the lack of third row offers a generous amount of cargo space for a midsize SUV. Behind the rear seats, you’ll find 39 cubic feet of storage, which expands to 75 cubic feet with the rear seats folded flat. The hands-free power liftgate makes loading and unloading effortless, adding to the SUV’s practicality.

The 2025 Mazda CX-70 PHEV is an excellent choice for drivers seeking a midsize SUV that combines performance, efficiency, cargo, and premium features. With its powerful plug-in hybrid system, luxurious interior, and engaging driving dynamics, the CX-70 PHEV strikes a compelling balance between practicality and excitement. It’s a standout option in the increasingly competitive PHEV market, appealing to those who value both style, substance, and most of all engagement.

Pricing estimated starting at $54,400

⚡

2025 Toyota RAV4 Prime (PHEV): The Best of Both Worlds

We first have to clear the air, Toyota has dropped the name Prime for its 2025 Plug-Hybrid model and is now just calling it a PHEV as customers were confused with the models and trim levels. Having that cleared up the 2025 Toyota RAV4 PHEV continues to set a high bar for plug-in hybrid SUVs, combining eco-friendly efficiency with robust performance and the versatility expected from Toyota's popular RAV4 lineup. Building on the success of the standard RAV4 Hybrid, the PHEV adds a larger battery and enhanced electric-only capabilities, making it a top choice for eco-conscious drivers seeking versatility, whether you’re seeking power, practicality, or a step toward electrification, the RAV4 PHEV delivers it all.

The RAV4 PHEV features a 2.5-liter four-cylinder engine paired with two electric motors, producing a combined output of 302 horsepower. This impressive powertrain enables the RAV4 PHEV to accelerate from 0 to 60 mph in just 5.7 seconds, making it the quickest RAV4 ever! All-wheel drive comes standard, providing excellent traction and stability conquering any road conditions that you may encounter. As impressive as the speed is in the RAV4 PHEV it is the efficiency where it stands alone at the top. Equipped with an 18.1-kWh battery, the RAV4 PHEV offers an EPA-estimated 42 miles of all-electric range, making it ideal not just for daily commuters like most PHEVs but for almost any trip within reason, without using gas. In hybrid mode, it delivers an impressive 94 MPGe combined and 38 mpg when operating solely as a hybrid, providing exceptional efficiency across the board. The RAV4 PHEV has really cracked the code on efficiency.

Inside, the RAV4 PHEV boasts a modern and functional cabin with quality materials and thoughtful design, nothing really captivating that stands out. It offers seating for five, with ample space for both passengers and cargo. Higher trims such as the SE includes a power-adjustable driver’s seat, a power liftgate, and wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto compatibility, delivering convenience and connectivity. While the XLE Trim adds synthetic leather upholstery, a larger infotainment screen, and an upgraded JBL premium audio system, wireless charging, and a head-up display and options of a panoramic sunroof, and heated and ventilated front seats can be added.

With up to 33.5 cubic feet of cargo space behind the rear seats and 63.2 cubic feet with them folded down, the RAV4 PHEV remains highly practical. Its spacious interior and configurable seating make it a great choice for families or those with active lifestyles. Along with the advanced safety features, including adaptive cruise control, lane-keeping assist, automatic emergency braking, and more, the RAV4 PHEV is a great package.

In conclusion the Toyota RAV4 PHEV is a well-rounded plug-in hybrid SUV that combines practicality, performance, and impressive efficiency. Whether you prioritize eco-friendly driving, advanced technology, or a versatile interior, the RAV4 Prime has you covered. It’s a smart choice for those looking to transition to electrified driving without compromising on reliability or everyday usability.

Starting Price $43,865

⚡

2025 Volvo XC60 Recharge Plug-In Hybrid: The Bold and Beautiful

The Volvo XC60 Recharge radiates beauty, even in a design a shade under 10 years and it could still go another 10, it is that beautiful. It masterfully blends Scandinavian elegance, eco-friendly technology, and practical utility. As part of Volvo’s push toward full electrification, the XC60 Recharge offers a premium driving experience while reducing its environmental footprint.

The XC60 Recharge is powered by a plug-in hybrid system that combines a turbocharged and supercharged 2.0-liter four-cylinder engine with an electric motor. Together, they produce an astonishing 455 horsepower and 523 pound-feet of torque, all of this power is controlled through an eight-speed automatic transmission catapulting the XC60 Recharge to 60 mph is a eye blinking 4.3 seconds, making it one of the most powerful SUVs in its class. The PHEV system allows for up to 35 miles of all-electric driving, more than enough to get you around town and for a slight excursion. No need to fret when the battery is depleted, the hybrid mode achieves a respectable 63 MPGe combined, when putting all this together you have a 560 mile range of convenience. Charging the XC60 Recharge is also convenient, with a Level 2 charger capable of replenishing the battery in about 3 hours.

The XC60 Recharge’s cabin is a study in minimalist luxury, featuring high-quality materials like real wood, metal, and leather-free upholstery options. Its seating for five is exceptionally comfortable, with ample space for both front and rear passengers, however cargo is slightly limited (22.4 cubic feet) for rear passenger comfort. The rear seats do fold down to expand the cargo area up to 63.3 cubic feet for added versatility, more than the Audi Q5 or Lexus NX. Staying with the inside Volvo has Google-based infotainment system that comes standard, featuring a 9-inch touchscreen, Google Maps integration, and voice-activated controls. Other tech highlights include best in class Bowers & Wilkins premium audio system, wireless smartphone charging, and standard driver-assistance features such as adaptive cruise control, lane-keeping assist, and automatic emergency braking.

One does have to emphasize Volvo’s reputation for safety, and XC60 Recharge is not different. It comes with Volvo’s latest Pilot Assist system, which offers semi-autonomous driving capabilities. Additional features like a 360-degree camera, blind-spot monitoring, and cross-traffic alert add to its appeal. True to Volvo’s heritage, the XC60 Recharge provides a balanced and composed ride. The standard all-wheel drive system ensures stability and confidence in all conditions, while the suspension offers a perfect blend of comfort and responsiveness. Its driving modes include an all-electric "Pure" mode, a hybrid mode, and a "Power" mode for spirited driving.

The 2025 Volvo XC60 Recharge Plug-In Hybrid is an excellent choice for drivers who value luxury, safety, and sustainability. With its robust performance, advanced technology, and eco-conscious design, it’s a standout in the crowded luxury compact SUV segment. Whether for family trips or urban commutes, the XC60 Recharge delivers a premium experience that aligns with Volvo’s commitment to a greener future. You will be hard pressed to find a more elegant PHEV on the market.

Starting Price: $59,345

⚡

2025 Jeep Wrangler 4xe: Old School Enhanced by Technology

Jeeps first attempt with a plug-in hybrid version of the iconic Wrangler combines classic off-road capability that it is known for with modern electrification, with hopes of generating new buyers and a better footprint.

This blend of both is like a summit meeting at the UN, a good start but needs to accomplish more. The Wrangler does deliver a strong performance overall, but its fuel efficiency, the one aspect it was aiming to improve does fall more one the disappointing side of the spectrum, especially for a PHEV. The Wrangler 4xe features a single powertrain: a turbocharged 2.0-liter four-cylinder engine paired with a 17-kWh battery and two electric motors, collectively producing a whopping 375 horsepower and 470 pound-feet of torque. Most will enjoy the endless amounts of torque due to the electric motors for those who will actually use the Wrangler for what it was designed for, in off-road situations where the Wrangler excels. Harnessing the power is an eight-speed automatic transmission with standard four-wheel drive. However, even with all that power and torque at your fingertips acceleration is not its strong suit, nor should it be reaching 60 mph in about 7 seconds.

As for fuel efficiency, the Wrangler 4xe falls short of expectations. It offers an electric-only range of approximately 22 miles before the gas engine activates. While EPA ratings for the 2025 model are not yet available, the nearly identical 2024 version achieves an EPA-estimated 49 MPGe in combined electric/hybrid driving and 20 mpg in hybrid mode alone. Charging the battery using a standard 120-volt outlet takes around 12 hours, but a 240-volt outlet or Level 2 charger reduces this to about 2.5 hours.

Inside, the Wrangler 4xe features a retro-inspired design with high-quality materials and distinctive trim options for each model. It provides comfortable seating for five, with generous headroom and legroom in both rows. Cargo space is also ample, offering 27.7 cubic feet behind the rear seats and 67.4 cubic feet with them folded down. The Wrangler 4xe lags behind newer competitors in ride quality and handling as one should suspect coming from a vehicle sculpted to hit the rougher side of life, not all is lost its on-road performance has improved over time, it still struggles with steering that feels unsettled and a rough ride.

Jeep has made a valiant effort with the Wrangler 4Xe and knows its buyers will most likely be hopping into these for as weekend warriors hitting the trail vs a daily commuter. Having that said, its a Wrangler, you forget about all of that when the top is off and you are in the middle of nowhere.

Pricing starts at $50,695

⚡

2025 Lincoln Corsair Grand Touring: Luxury Meets Plug-In Hybrid Performance

The Corsair Grand Touring is one of the most overlooked and underrated Plug-In Hybrids, anyone who is the market for a PHEV should have this close to the top of their list. A premium compact plug-in hybrid SUV that combines elegant design, advanced technology, and efficient performance. Offering a refined driving experience with a blend of electric and gasoline power, providing a versatile option for those seeking luxury and sustainability in a single package.

The 2025 Corsair Grand Touring is powered by Ford’s 2.5-liter Atkinson-cycle four-cylinder engine paired with an electric motor, producing a combined 250 horsepower and 275 lb-ft. This powertrain provides smooth acceleration and impressive power for a compact SUV, with the electric motor contributing to a quiet and refined ride. Controlling all this power is a continuously variable transmission (CVT), allowing for smooth transitions between electric and gasoline power, which enhances both responsiveness and overall driving efficiency. The electric motor alone can account for somewhere around 28 miles, making perfect for daily commutes or every day driving. Switch the toggle to hybrid mode and it achieves an EPA-estimated 42 mpg in the city and 39 mpg on the highway, providing excellent efficiency for long trips or highway driving. Those are Prius numbers. Let the wins keep coming with the Corsair being fully charged in approximately 3.5 hours using a Level 2 charger.

As good as the efficiency is, this is after all a Lincoln and it exudes luxury, with premium materials and sophisticated design elements. The cabin offers endless amounts of quality and ample space for both passengers and cargo, with seating for five in plush, comfortable seats. The layout is both functional and elegant, featuring soft-touch surfaces, ambient lighting, and high-quality materials throughout. You feel important while inside. Standard features include a 12.3-inch digital display for the instrument cluster, a 13.2-inch infotainment touchscreen with Lincoln's SYNC 4 system, and wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto compatibility. The infotainment system is intuitive and user-friendly, offering easy access to navigation, media, and vehicle controls. Optional upgrades, such as a panoramic sunroof, a Revel audio system, and massaging front seats, further enhance the luxury experience.

The only aspect that may trump the luxury and refinement in the Corsair would be the comprehensive suite of advanced safety and technology crawling all over it. Lincoln's Co-Pilot 360 suite includes standard adaptive cruise control, lane-keeping assist, blind-spot monitoring, automatic emergency braking, and a 360-degree camera system. Additional available safety options include parking assist and a head-up display. For convenience and connectivity, the Corsair Grand Touring offers a range of tech features, such as an available wireless charging pad, a hands-free liftgate, and multiple USB ports throughout the cabin. The SUV’s advanced driver-assistance systems provide added peace of mind, ensuring a safe and confident driving experience. Continuing with innovation and quality the suspension system is tuned for comfort, absorbing road imperfections without sacrificing handling or responsiveness. While this happening the cabin remains exceptionally quiet, contributing to the serene atmosphere. The 2025 Lincoln Corsair Grand Touring offers a smooth and comfortable ride, with the ability to switch between electric and gasoline power for optimal performance. Beyond the luxury the Corsair is practical with 27.6 cubic feet of cargo space behind the rear seats and 57.6 cubic feet when the seats are folded down, the Corsair offers ample room for luggage, groceries, or outdoor gear. The hands-free lift gate provides added convenience, allowing easy access to the cargo area.

The 2025 Lincoln Corsair Grand Touring strikes an ideal balance between luxury, performance, and sustainability. With its powerful plug-in hybrid powertrain, elegant interior, and advanced technology, it’s a standout choice for those looking for a refined compact SUV that also embraces eco-friendly driving. Whether you're commuting in electric mode or taking longer trips in hybrid mode, the Corsair delivers a smooth, efficient, and comfortable ride for all passengers.

Pricing Starting at $54,365

⚡

2025 Dodge Hornet PHEV: Spunky but Expensive

The Dodge Hornet PHEV crossover represents Dodge’s effort to adapt its muscle-car legacy to a modern era by offering a smaller, more efficient package while retaining the brand’s signature performance-oriented ethos. Designed for changing times, it blends sporty design, engaging driving dynamics, and hybrid efficiency to cater to a broader audience without sacrificing its bold identity. The impact SUV features a turbocharged 1.3-liter four-cylinder engine paired with an electric motor and a 15.5-kWh battery pack, delivering a total output of 258 horsepower but has a “Power Shot” function that temporarily boosts output to 288 horsepower and 383 pound-feet of torque. All-wheel drive is standard, and the vehicle is equipped with a six-speed automatic transmission. All of that power keeps inline with Dodge’s ethos as the Hornet blasts to 60mph from stand still is 5.5 seconds, the sharp steering and its sporty reflexes act as a catalyst keeping the Hornet light on its feet as it bounces around town, a rarity in the PHEV league.

Like most things with power it comes at a cost, the Hornet PHEV the worst fuel economy in the segment, 29 mpg combined or 77 MPGe when factoring in electric use. . Despite these drawbacks, the Hornet Hybrid offers an EV-only range of 31–32 miles, appealing to those with short commutes. On a 200-mile highway test, it achieved 27 mpg and ran on electricity for 31 miles before switching to gas. A Level 2 charger fully recharges the battery in about 2.5 hours.

Inside, the Dodge Hornet PHEV boasts a modern and driver-focused cabin with high-quality materials and advanced technology. Featuring heated front seats, an eight-way power driver’s seat, a 10.3-inch Uconnect 5 infotainment system, dual-zone climate control, and a 12.3-inch digital gauge cluster. Upgrading to the R/T Plus adds leather ventilated seating, a sunroof, a premium Harman Kardon sound system, and wireless charging but pushes the price into premium territory with the likes of BMW and Audi. The Hornet's compact size also limits headroom, rear passenger space, and cargo capacity compared to rivals.

Driver-assistance features include automated emergency braking with pedestrian detection, blind-spot monitoring, and parking sensors. The optional Tech package adds adaptive cruise control with lane-centering, a driver attention monitor, and a 360-degree camera system.

The Hornet Hybrid offers solid tech, impressive standard features, and fun driving dynamics, making it a viable choice for shoppers prioritizing performance and hybrid benefits over spaciousness. The Hornet PHEV stands out for its athletic driving dynamics, with responsive steering and a well-tuned suspension that balances comfort and sportiness. It’s designed to appeal to drivers who want a compact SUV that offers engaging performance while embracing the benefits of hybrid technology. If it was only priced better.

Starting Price $42,530

⚡

Porsche Cayenne E-Hybrid: Luxury Meets Electrified Performance

Porsche’s motto, “There is no Substitute”, may have placed Porsche in a bind with this PHEV option. The 2024 Porsche Cayenne E-Hybrid blends Porsche's signature performance with plug-in hybrid efficiency, delivering a sporty yet eco-conscious SUV experience. Updated for 2025, the Cayenne E-Hybrid boasts refreshed styling, improved interior technology, and a powerful hybrid powertrain.

At its core, the Cayenne E-Hybrid features a 3.0-liter turbocharged V6 engine paired with an electric motor and a larger 25.9-kWh battery pack, generating a combined 463 horsepower and 479 pound feet of torque ready for you at all times. This setup enables the SUV to accelerate from 0 to 60 mph in blitzkrieg attacking speed 4.6 seconds and will keeping advancing until its hits it limiter at 158mph. While the Cayenne E-Hybrid is designed for efficiency, it doesn’t compromise on handling. Porsche’s adaptive suspension and dynamic chassis control provide a comfortable ride with sporty dynamics, whether on highways or twisty roads. All-wheel drive is standard, and the Cayenne E-Hybrid offers impressive towing capability of up to 7,700 pounds.

The electric-only range is anywhere from 40 to 48 miles making it suitable to sit at the table with some pure EVs and hold a conversation. Charging the 25.9-kWh battery with a Level 2 charger takes about 2.5 hours, using the E-Hybrid’s 11-kW onboard charger. For 2025, the Cayenne E-Hybrid gained an updated exterior design, as well as a new infotainment system. This builds on its refined, premium cabin and its usable 55.1 cubic feet of cargo space, accessed via a standard power liftgate.

Inside, the Cayenne E-Hybrid offers a luxurious and tech-forward cabin. A 12.3-inch curved display houses the updated Porsche Communication Management (PCM) infotainment system, which includes wireless Apple CarPlay, Android Auto, and an optional passenger display for entertainment and navigation. Premium materials, ergonomic seating, and an available suite of customizations enhance the driving experience. Standard driver-assistance features include adaptive cruise control, lane-keeping assist, and automated emergency braking, with optional upgrades like night vision assist and a 360-degree camera system as one would expect from a Porsche.

The 2025 Porsche Cayenne E-Hybrid caters to those who seek a high-performance luxury SUV with the added benefits of plug-in hybrid technology, balancing thrilling performance with improved fuel efficiency and reduced emissions with substituting anything in between.

Pricing starting at $91,700

⚡

2025 KIA Sportage PHEV & Hyundai Tucson PHEV: Fraternal Twins

Fraternal twins share about 50% of their DNA and can be the same or different depending on where you look. Considering that KIA and Hyundai are sister companies and tend to share a number of their secrets it all comes down to which one speaks to you best. In this case both Hyundai Tucson and Kia Sportage share the same platform, powertrains, and even the same basic dimensions. So what are the differences and which one is for you?

Let’s be clear here neither of these vehicles are built for speed, they offer similar powertrains and the majority of buyers will be pleased with the acceleration of both of these vehicles. The Sportage PHEV has a 1.4L turbocharged four-cylinder engine and an electric motor putting out a combined 261 horsepower and 258lb-ft of torque, paired with a six-speed automatic transmission pushing the standard front-wheel drive to 60 mph in respectable 7.1 seconds and is able to produce confidence with passing and merging on the freeway. The Tucson Hybrid has the same 1.4L turbocharged four-cylinder paired with an electric motor but this one found an extra 7 ponies in the stable making 268 horsepower but torque staying the same at 258lb-ft. Mated to the same six-speed automatic transmission the power is now being put through the standard all-wheel drive system, the extra weight and gear movements has the Hyundai losing in the drag race to 60 by 0.5 seconds reaching it in 7.6 seconds. It still has plenty of pep, performing well in all driving situations.

Continuing on with the minor differences or similarities depending on how you would like to view it. Where the Tucson lost in the sprint 0-60mph it gains a win in charging. When using a 240v takes 1.7 hours to charge while the Sportage PHEV comes in at 2hours, again not much of a difference. Once on the road the Sportage PHEV sips fuel as one would do with water when crossing an endless desert. It it earns a 34mpg or 84 MPGe and solid 34 miles of pure EV range, enough for getting to and from most places during the day. The Tucson with its all wheel drive gets an impressive 33mpg and 80MPGe and roughly 33 miles of pure EV range.

When on the road the Hyundai Tucson PHEV has a stable and confident ride and feels smooth when driving. The steering is precise and drivers will have no trouble maneuvering this SUV around tight corners or in small parking lots. The Sportage PHEV also has a calm and easy ride, and it handles well around corners and brakes confidently. That said, the Tucson just feels a bit more athletic and engaging.

The interior features of the Sportage Hybrid and the Tucson Hybrid are so closely matched that Sherlock himself would be hard pressed to differentiate the two. Having said that the Tucson PHEV does offer a few features missing in the Sportage PHEV. Tucson's cabin is stylish, with plenty of soft-touch surfaces and good build quality. It's also insulated well from outside road noise. The top-tier Limited trim has genuine leather upholstery, an option not found in the Sportage PHEV. The Tucson PHEV also has a larger, 10.25-inch touch screen with nice graphics, and there is no lag time after user inputs. There's also a 4.2-inch digital instrument display with an analog gauge cluster, wired Apple CarPlay and Android Auto, wireless device charging, satellite radio, dual-zone automatic climate control, proximity keyless entry and push-button start. The interior of the Sportage is still well-built, with just a few hard plastics here and there that don't detract from the overall look and feel. Most of the materials feel high-end, and cloth upholstery comes standard. The Sportage PHEV comes standard with an 8-inch touch screen that boasts crisp graphics and a good response time. There's also a 12.2-inch digital instrument cluster, wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto, Bluetooth, dual-zone automatic climate control and remote keyless entry.

Even though they are essentially identical when it comes to cargo capacity the Tucson PHEV edges out the Sportage PHEV only when the seats are folded, when up the Sportage is larger. The Kia has 39.5 cubic feet of room behind the rear row and a maximum of 73.7 cubic feet with that row folded down. The Hyundai has 38.7 cubic feet behind the rear seats and a total of 74.5 cubic feet when those seats are not in use.

The 2025 KIA Sportage PHEV has a low starting price of $28,590. The front-wheel-drive LX base trim is well-equipped with an 8-inch touch screen, cloth seats, Apple CarPlay, Android Auto and a nice list of standard safety features. All-wheel drive is optional on that base LX trim and standard on the others. The top-of-the-line SX Prestige trim comes in at over $37,000. The 2025 Hyundai Tucson PHEV base model costs $32,575, and perks include all-wheel drive, heated front seats and navigation. The top-tier Limited trim will run you $39,715. These are the top two vehicles in their class, and they're closely matched in almost every category. The Kia is victorious when it comes to price, reliability and fuel economy. The Hyundai Tucson PHEV wins out when it comes to safety, cargo space, ride quality, interior quality and interior features. Both KIA and Hyundai have done their homework with these vehicles, if you are looking at both I do not envy your predicament.

Pricing starts:

KIA Sportage PHEV $28,590

Hyundai Tucson PHEV $32,575

⚡

2025 Kia Sorento Plug-in Hybrid: A Family-Friendly Electrified SUV

In the bustling world of midsize SUVs, the 2025 KIA Sorento PHEV stands out as a compelling blend of family practicality, advanced technology, and eco-friendly innovation. Imagine a family of six or even your own family for that matter embarking on your next great adventure whether it's a cross-country road trip or a daily school run the Sorento's efficient powertrain and modern amenities is the ideal steed for your journey.

Under the hood, the Sorento PHEV boasts a 1.6-liter turbocharged engine paired with an electric motor and a 13.8-kWh battery. Together, they deliver a respectable 261 horsepower, ensuring enough pep to tackle highway merges or steep mountain roads with confidence. The 6-speed automatic transmission shifts smoothly, while the standard all-wheel drive offers reassurance when the weather takes a turn or the path ahead is unpaved. On electric power alone, this SUV can travel up to 32 miles, making it ideal for market runs or just wanting to be nicer to the environment by not burning a drop of fuel. For longer journeys, its hybrid system provides an EPA-estimated 79 MPGe in combined driving and 34 mpg in gas-only mode, proving that efficiency doesn’t have to come at the cost of capability. With a towing capacity of 2,000 pounds, it can easily pull a small trailer, perfect for a family getaway.

As you step inside, the Sorento PHEV greets you with a thoughtfully designed cabin. There’s room for six passengers, with plush second-row captain’s chairs offering comfort for the kids (or adults) and a third row that’s handy for extra friends or family members. While the third row is best suited for younger passengers, the overall seating layout is perfect for a growing family. The interior materials are soft to the touch, giving the SUV an upscale feel. For the tech-savvy parent or teen, the 10.25-inch touchscreen provides an intuitive interface with wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto, ensuring playlists, maps, and podcasts are always within reach. Meanwhile, the 12.3-inch digital gauge cluster keeps the driver informed with a sleek, modern display. On higher trims, extras like a Bose premium sound system, panoramic sunroof, and wireless charging add a layer of luxury.

On every journey, peace of mind is paramount, and the Sorento PHEV delivers with a suite of advanced safety features. From adaptive cruise control that makes long drives less taxing to blind-spot monitoring that adds confidence in crowded parking lots, this SUV is designed with family safety in mind. For those who want even more, an optional 360-degree camera system and semi-autonomous driving capabilities provide cutting-edge assistance.

The Sorento PHEV doesn’t just perform well does it with style. With Kia’s signature “tiger nose” grille, bold body lines, and sleek LED lighting, it strikes a perfect balance between rugged capability and modern elegance. Whether parked at the soccer field or gliding through city streets, it’s bound to attract admiring glances. The 19-inch alloy wheels help with giving the SUV a commanding stance. When it comes to cargo, the Sorento PHEV offers plenty of flexibility. Whether it’s groceries, sports gear, or luggage for a weekend trip, the 75.5 cubic feet of cargo space with the second and third rows folded ensures nothing gets left behind.

The Sorento PHEV comes in two well-equipped trims, starting around $50,000 for the SX-P and climbing to $55,000 for the premium SX-P Prestige. Both trims come loaded with features, so even the base model feels luxurious. While it may be pricier than some competitors, its generous list of amenities and impressive hybrid tech make it a worthwhile investment for families seeking efficiency without compromise.

The 2025 Kia Sorento PHEV isn’t just a vehicle—it’s a partner for families navigating the complexities of modern life. Whether it’s taking kids to soccer practice on electric power or embarking on a cross-country road trip with hybrid efficiency, this SUV is ready for anything. With its blend of practicality, style, and cutting-edge technology, the Sorento PHEV proves that going green can still mean going big.

Pricing starting around $50,000

⚡

2025 Volvo XC90 Recharge: Sanctuary on Wheels

The 2025 Volvo XC90 emerges with a refreshed look and feel, seamlessly blending modernity with its signature elegance. The exterior has been subtly reimagined to mirror the design language of the upcoming all-electric EX90. A smoother, more cohesive aesthetic is achieved with redesigned air intakes, a sleeker front bumper, and reworked hood and fenders. The aging headlamps have been replaced by a striking set of T-shaped Matrix LED lights, while the taillamps now sport a crisp, new LED signature. To complete the visual upgrade, Volvo offers a choice of sophisticated 20-, 21-, and 22-inch wheels.

Underneath the XC90 Recharge’s sophisticated exterior lies a plug-in hybrid powertrain that marries power with eco-conscious performance. Its 2.0-liter turbocharged and supercharged four-cylinder engine, combined with an electric motor and a 19.8-kWh battery, delivers an impressive 455 horsepower. The result? Smooth, powerful acceleration that ensures effortless highway cruising and responsive handling in city traffic. On electric power alone, the XC90 Recharge can travel up to 38 miles, perfect for a silent and emissions-free school run or commute. When the gas engine joins the party, the SUV achieves a combined 58 MPGe efficiency, making it one of the greener options in the luxury three-row SUV segment. Thanks to all-wheel drive, the XC90 Recharge is equally adept at handling snowy driveways or gravel paths leading to a secluded cabin. Charging the XC90 Recharge is as seamless as its design. On a Level 2 charger, the battery recharges in about 5 hours, one of slowest there is, ideally making it an overnight or during a workday charge. Its combination of electric and gas power eliminates range anxiety, giving families the freedom to travel wherever adventure calls.

Stepping inside, the XC90 embraces tech-driven luxury and welcomes you with its hallmark Scandinavian design ethos—clean lines, premium materials, and an emphasis on light and space. The cabin is a harmonious blend of natural wood inlays, Nappa leather upholstery, and sustainable materials like wool blends, offering a serene escape from the chaos of the outside world. Volvo's already impressive Google-powered system now boasts an intuitive “contextual bar,” displaying commonly used apps on the home page for easier navigation. This update, inspired by the EX90, ensures fewer taps and smoother interactions. The nucleus of the interior is a new 11.2-inch freestanding central display, replacing the old dashboard-integrated screen. Tech enthusiasts will appreciate the 21% boost in pixel density, delivering sharper visuals. The XC90 Recharge seats up to seven passengers which means the whole family can join in and ride in comfort, with spacious first and second rows ensuring ample legroom. Even the third row, often an afterthought in SUVs, is accommodating enough for adults on shorter trips. Audiophiles will revel in the optional Bowers & Wilkins sound system, which transforms the cabin into a concert hall.

With 85.7 cubic feet of cargo space with the seats folded, the XC90 Recharge has room for everything from suitcases to soccer gear. Volvo has also attended to practicality, responding to customer feedback with thoughtful touches. The cabin features enhanced sound insulation for a quieter ride, an additional cup holder for convenience, and a repositioned wireless charging pad neatly tucked behind the central tunnel.

Under the surface, the XC90’s driving dynamics see a significant improvement with the introduction of Frequency Selective Damping. This advanced suspension system adapts to your driving style, softening for a smoother ride at lower speeds and stiffening for greater control and stability during spirited driving.

With these updates, the 2025 XC90 combines refined aesthetics, cutting-edge technology, and improved performance to deliver a premium driving experience tailored to modern needs. The king of elegance has returned with more tech and ambiance then ever before.

Pricing starts at $58,540

Conclusion

Hopefully, this has expanded your perspective and offered clarity as you weigh your options, while also encouraging you to explore possibilities you might not have previously considered. The world of plug-in hybrids is rich and varied, with each model tailored to meet specific strengths and cater to diverse lifestyles. To make the best decision, start by reflecting on your unique wants and needs.

What matters most to you—range, efficiency, performance, or features? Are you prioritizing daily commuting, long-distance travel, or perhaps a balance of both? By creating a detailed list of your priorities, you'll be equipped to navigate the many choices confidently and find a plug-in hybrid that aligns perfectly with your life. Rest assured, with thoughtful consideration, you can’t go wrong in making an informed and fulfilling choice.
